content:
There's a number of routes to set the ring tone.

via Thunderbird app settings

1a. Nagivate to settings > account > notifications 1b. Click new mail ringtone. Assign a tone

2a. Nagivate to settings > account > notifications 2b. Click "messages" under Notification Categories header 2c. Click "ring" and choose a tone

via Android settings

3a. Navigate to Android settings > apps 3b. Click Thunderbird app 3c. Click manage notifications 3d. Click "messages" under email account header 3e. Click "Ring" and assign a tone.
creator: platform34

content:
Do you have a large number of messages within your inbox (>500) or many email accounts. The app is known to slow down in such conditions.

The developers are refactoring the message list which may help once released. See the below links for released issue reports.

https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues/10373#issuecomment-3810077139 https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues/10409#issuecomment-3811149422 https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues/10373#issuecomment-3778691141

Do you have the feature enabled to use recipient names from your Android contacts app. Does performance improve if you disable this?
